volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 3.5.787 - Section 3.6.854b)

sections.label: Section 3.5.787
sections.translation: On the contrary , they shall move towards you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.787b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , for my own part , I don ’ t think so .

sections.label: Section 3.5.788
sections.translation: If they do move nearer to you , what will you do ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.788b
sections.translation: I ’ ll retire . But , old fellow , if ever I catch you in the city ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.790
sections.original: numquam hercle quisquam me lenonem dixerit ,
sections.translation: never again , upon my faith , shall any one call me a Procurer , if I don ’ t give you some most disagreable sport .

sections.label: Section 3.5.792
sections.original: Facito istuc quod minitare ; sed nunc interim ,
sections.translation: Do what you threaten . But now , in the meantime , if you do touch them , a heavy punishment shall be inflicted on you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.794
sections.translation: How heavy , in fact ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.794b
sections.translation: Just as much as is sufficient for a Procurer .

sections.label: Section 3.5.795
sections.original: Minacias ego flocci non faciam tuas ,
sections.translation: These threats of yours I don ’ t value one straw ; I certainly shall seize them both this instant without your leave .

sections.label: Section 3.5.796b
sections.translation: Just touch them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.797
sections.translation: By my troth , I surely will touch them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.797b
sections.translation: You will touch them , but do you know with what result ? Go then , Turbalio , with all haste , and bring hither from out of the house two cudgels .

sections.label: Section 3.5.799b
sections.translation: Cudgels ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.799c
sections.translation: Aye , good ones ; make haste speedily . ( TURBALIO goes in .)

sections.label: Section 3.5.800
sections.original: ego te hodie faxo recte acceptum , ut dignus es .
sections.translation: I ’ ll let you have a reception this day in proper style , as you are deserving of .

sections.label: Section 3.5.801
sections.original: Eheu , scelestus galeam in navi perdidi ;
sections.translation: Alas ! cursedly unfortunate . I lost my headpiece in the ship ; it would now have been handy for me , if it had been saved . ( To DAEMONES .) May I at least address these women ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.803b
sections.translation: You may not ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.5.804_805
sections.original: ehem , optime edepol eccum clavator ádvenit .
sections.translation: ( TURBALIO enters bringing two cudgels .) Well now , by my faith , look , the cudgel-man is coming very opportunely here .

sections.label: Section 3.5.806
sections.original: Illud quidem edepol tinnimentum est auribus .
sections.translation: By my troth , this surely is a tingling for my ears .

sections.label: Section 3.5.807
sections.original: Age accipe illinc alteram clavam , Sparax .
sections.translation: Come , Sparax , do you take this other cudgel . ( Giving him one .) Come , take your stand , one on one side , the other on one other . Take your stations both of you . ( They stand with lifted cudgels on each side of he altar .) Just so . Now then attend to me :

sections.label: Section 3.5.810
sections.original: si hercle illic illas hodie digito tetigerit
sections.translation: if , I ’ faith , that fellow there should this day touch these women with his finger against their inclination , if you don ’ t give him a reception [*] with these cudgels even to that degree that he shan ’ t know which way he is to get home , you are undone , both of you . If he shall call for any one , do you make answer to this fellow in their stead .

sections.label: Section 3.5.815
sections.original: sin ipse abire hínc volet , quantum potest
sections.translation: But if he himself shall attempt to get away from here , that instant , as hard a you can , lay on to his legs with your sticks .

sections.label: Section 3.5.817
sections.original: Etiam me abire hinc non sinent ?
sections.translation: Are they not even to allow me to go away from here ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.817b
sections.translation: I ’ ve said sufficient . And when that servant comes here with his master , he that has gone to fetch his master , do you at once go home .

sections.label: Section 3.5.820
sections.translation: Attend to this with great diligence , will you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.821
sections.original: Heu hercle , ne istic fana mutantur cito :
sections.translation: O rare , by my troth , the Temple here is surely changed all of a sudden ; this is now the Temple of Hercules [*] which was that of Venus before ; in such fashion has the old fellow planted two statues here with clubs . I ’ faith , I don ’ t know now whither in the world I shall fly from here ;

sections.label: Section 3.5.825
sections.original: ita nunc mi utrumque saevit , et terra et mare .
sections.translation: so greatly are they both raging now against me , both land and sea . Palaestra !

sections.label: Section 3.5.826b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.826c
sections.translation: Away with you , there is a misunderstanding between us ; that , indeed , is not my Palaestra [*] that answers . Harkye , Ampelisca .

sections.label: Section 3.5.828b
sections.translation: Beware of a mishap , will you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.829
sections.original: Vltro te . signa ut homines satis recte monent .
sections.translation: So far as they can , the worthless fellows advise me rightly enough .

sections.label: Section 3.5.830
sections.original: sed vobis dico , heús vos , num molestiaest ,
sections.translation: ( Aloud .) But , harkye , I ask you , whether it is any harm to you for me to come nearer to these women ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.831b
sections.translation: Why none at all to ourselves .

sections.label: Section 3.5.832
sections.translation: Will there be any harm to myself ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.832b
sections.translation: None at all , if you only take care .

sections.label: Section 3.5.833
sections.translation: What is it that I ’ m to take care against ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.833b
sections.translation: Why , look you , against a heavy mishap .

sections.label: Section 3.5.834
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , do let me approach them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.834b
sections.translation: Approach them , if you like .

sections.label: Section 3.5.835
sections.original: Bene hercle factum . habeo vobis gratiam .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , obligingly done ; I return you thanks , I ’ ll go nearer to them .

sections.label: Section 3.5.836b
sections.translation: Do you stand there on the spot , where you are .

sections.label: Section 3.5.837
sections.translation: By my faith , I ’ ve come scurvily off in many ways . Still , I ’ m resolved to get the better of them this day by constantly besieging them .

sections.label: Section 3.6.839
sections.original: Meamne ílle amicam leno vi , violentia
sections.translation: And did the Procurer attempt by force and violence

sections.label: Section 3.6.840
sections.translation: to drag my mistress away from the altar of Venus ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.840b
sections.translation: Even so .

sections.label: Section 3.6.841
sections.translation: Why didn ’ t you kill him on the instant ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.841b
sections.translation: I hadn ’ t a sword .

sections.label: Section 3.6.842
sections.translation: You should have taken either a stick or a stone .

sections.label: Section 3.6.842b
sections.translation: What ! ought I to have pelted this most villanous fellow with stones like a dog ? ⋯

sections.label: Section 3.6.844
sections.original: Nunc pol ego perii , Plesidippus eccum adest .
sections.translation: By my troth , but I ’ m undone now ; see , here ’ s Plesidippus ;

sections.label: Section 3.6.845
sections.original: converret iam hic me totum cum pulvisculo .
sections.translation: he ’ ll be sweeping me away altogether this moment with the dust .

sections.label: Section 3.6.846
sections.original: Etiamne in ara tunc sedebant mulieres ,
sections.translation: Were the damsels sitting on the altar even then when you set out to come to me ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.847b
sections.translation: Yes , and now they are sitting in the same place .

sections.label: Section 3.6.848
sections.translation: Who is now protecting them there ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.848b
sections.translation: Some old gentleman , I don ’ t know who , a neighbour of the Temple of Venus — he gave very kind assistance ;

sections.label: Section 3.6.850
sections.original: is nunc cum servis servat . ego mandaveram .
sections.translation: he is now protecting them with his servants — I committed them to his charge .

sections.label: Section 3.6.851
sections.original: Duc me ad lenonem recta . ubi illic est homo ?
sections.translation: Lead me straight to the Procurer . Where is this fellow ?

sections.label: Section 3.6.852
sections.translation: Health to you .

sections.label: Section 3.6.852b
sections.translation: I want none of your healths . Make your choice quickly , whether you lad rather be seized by your throat wrenched [*] , or be dragged along ; choose whichever you please , while you may .

sections.label: Section 3.6.854b
sections.translation: I wish for neither .
